questions.prompt: The characteristic impedance of a lossless transmission line is:
choices: A. Z₀ = √(L'/C') where L' and C' are inductance and capacitance per unit length
questions.explanation: Lossless line: Z₀ = √(L'/C'). For lossy: Z₀ = √((R'+jωL')/(G'+jωC')).

questions.prompt: The reflection coefficient Γ_L at a load Z_L on a line with characteristic impedance Z₀ is:
questions.explanation: Γ_L = (Z_L − Z₀)/(Z_L + Z₀). Matched load Z_L = Z₀ → Γ_L = 0 (no reflection). Open circuit Z_L = ∞ → Γ = +1. Short Z_L = 0 → Γ = −1.

questions.prompt: The voltage standing wave ratio (VSWR) is defined as:
questions.explanation: VSWR = V_max/V_min = (1 + |Γ|)/(1 − |Γ|). Ranges from 1 (matched) to ∞ (total reflection). Perfect match: VSWR = 1.

questions.prompt: The propagation constant γ of a transmission line is:
questions.explanation: γ = √((R'+jωL')(G'+jωC')) = α + jβ. α = attenuation (Np/m); β = phase constant (rad/m). Lossless: α = 0, β = ω√(L'C').

questions.prompt: For a lossless transmission line, the phase velocity is:
questions.explanation: v_p = ω/β = 1/√(L'C'). For air-filled coaxial: v_p = c.

questions.prompt: A quarter-wave transformer (λ/4 section) with impedance Z₁ matching source Z₀ to load Z_L requires:
questions.explanation: Quarter-wave transformer: Z₁ = √(Z₀ Z_L). The λ/4 section transforms Z_L to Z₀²/Z_L at the source end.

questions.prompt: The input impedance of a lossless transmission line of length l with load Z_L is:
choices: A. Z_in = Z₀(Z_L + jZ₀ tan βl)/(Z₀ + jZ_L tan βl)
questions.explanation: Z_in = Z₀(Z_L + jZ₀ tan βl)/(Z₀ + jZ_L tan βl). Special cases: l = λ/4 → Z_in = Z₀²/Z_L; l = λ/2 → Z_in = Z_L.

questions.prompt: For a short-circuit terminated lossless line (Z_L = 0), the input impedance is:
choices: A. Z_in = jZ₀ tan(βl) — purely reactive
questions.explanation: Z_L = 0: Z_in = jZ₀ tan(βl). At l = λ/4: Z_in = ∞ (open circuit equivalent). Purely reactive — no power dissipated.

questions.prompt: The Smith chart is primarily used for:
choices: B. Graphically determining impedance, reflection coefficient, and matching network design
questions.explanation: Smith chart: graphical tool for impedance transformation, reflection coefficient, VSWR, and matching network design for transmission lines.

questions.prompt: On the Smith chart, the center of the chart represents:
questions.explanation: Smith chart center: Γ = 0, normalized impedance z = 1 + j0 (perfect match). Short: left edge; open: right edge.

questions.prompt: Standing waves on a transmission line are caused by:
choices: B. Reflection from a mismatched load (|Γ| > 0)
questions.explanation: Standing waves = superposition of forward and reflected waves. Reflection coefficient |Γ| > 0 whenever Z_L ≠ Z₀.

questions.prompt: Return loss (RL) related to reflection coefficient Γ is:
questions.explanation: Return loss RL = −20 log₁₀|Γ| dB. Higher RL = less reflection. Perfect match: RL = ∞; total reflection: RL = 0 dB.

questions.prompt: A 50 Ω line is terminated with Z_L = 150 Ω. What is |Γ|?
Telegrapher's equations describe:
choices: A. Voltage and current as functions of position and time on a transmission line
B. Power flow in free space
D. Signal delays in digital circuits
questions.explanation: Telegrapher's equations: ∂V/∂z = −(R' + jωL')I; ∂I/∂z = −(G' + jωC')V. They govern V(z) and I(z) along a line.

questions.prompt: A λ/2 (half-wavelength) transmission line with load Z_L has input impedance:
questions.explanation: At l = λ/2: tan(βl) = tan(π) = 0 → Z_in = Z_L. Impedance repeats every half-wavelength.

questions.prompt: A stub matching network uses a short-circuited or open-circuited stub of length l to:
choices: A. Absorb power and increase VSWR
B. Add a pure susceptance to cancel the reactive part of the load and achieve matching
D. Increase the line length only
questions.explanation: Stub tuner: adds pure susceptance (jB) to cancel the imaginary part of the load admittance, achieving impedance match.

questions.prompt: The maximum power transfer on a transmission line occurs when:
choices: B. Z_L = Z₀* (complex conjugate of source)
questions.explanation: Maximum power transfer: Z_L = Z_S* (source conjugate match). For real Z₀: Z_L = Z₀. This gives Γ = 0 (if Z_S = Z₀).

questions.prompt: A coaxial cable has inner conductor radius a and outer conductor radius b. The characteristic impedance is:
questions.explanation: Coaxial cable: Z₀ = (60/√ε_r) ln(b/a) Ω. For air dielectric (ε_r=1) and b/a ≈ 3.59: Z₀ = 50 Ω (standard coax).

questions.prompt: Which conditions result in total reflection (|Γ| = 1) on a transmission line? (Select ALL that apply.)
questions.explanation: Total reflection: open (Γ=+1), short (Γ=−1), and purely reactive load (|Γ|=1, power reflected entirely). Matched: Γ=0.

questions.prompt: In the context of transmission lines, the term 'dispersion' refers to:
choices: A. Signal spreading in time because phase velocity varies with frequency
questions.explanation: Dispersion: phase velocity varies with frequency → signal components travel at different speeds → pulse spreading in time.

questions.prompt: The wavelength on a transmission line with phase velocity v_p at frequency f is:
questions.explanation: λ = v_p/f. If the line has a dielectric (ε_r > 1), v_p < c and λ is shorter than in free space.

questions.prompt: Lumped-circuit models for transmission lines are valid when the physical length is:
choices: A. Much greater than the wavelength (l >> λ)
B. Much less than the wavelength (l << λ)
questions.explanation: Lumped model valid for l << λ (typically l < λ/10). For l ~ λ or longer, distributed (transmission line) analysis is needed.

questions.prompt: Insertion loss of a two-port network is defined as:
questions.explanation: Insertion loss: IL = 10 log₁₀(P_in/P_out) dB. Note: IL ≥ 0 dB (passive networks). Often expressed as −20 log₁₀|S₂₁|.

questions.prompt: The Heaviside condition for a distortionless transmission line requires:
choices: B. R'/L' = G'/C' (loss balanced between electric and magnetic)
D. The line length equals λ/4
questions.explanation: Heaviside (distortionless): R'/L' = G'/C'. Then α = R'√(C'/L') and v_p = 1/√(L'C') are constant with frequency → no dispersion.

questions.prompt: A matched load on a transmission line means:
choices: C. ZL = Z0 so reflection coefficient is zero
questions.explanation: When ZL equals Z0, Γ=(ZL-Z0)/(ZL+Z0)=0, so no reflected wave is produced and VSWR equals 1.
